followupAnswerBank.question: How does a missing HANA index cause AMDP performance issues?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Missing HANA index AMDP SQLScript mein accessed bade tables pe TABLE SCAN cause karta hai. Jab AMDP BSEG ko BUKRS aur GJAHR se filter karta hai, un columns pe composite index ke bina, HANA ko sab rows scan karne padte hain — bade clients ke liye potentially hundreds of millions. Index ke saath, HANA directly relevant rows seek karta hai. EXPLAIN PLAN TABLE SCAN vs INDEX SEARCH reveal karta hai. Fix hai DBA ke saath coordinated aur write performance pe impact ke liye tested frequently filtered columns pe composite index add karna.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A missing HANA index causes a TABLE SCAN on large tables accessed in AMDP SQLScript. When an AMDP filters BSEG by BUKRS and GJAHR, without a composite index on those columns, HANA must scan all rows — potentially hundreds of millions for large clients. With an index, HANA directly seeks to the relevant rows. EXPLAIN PLAN reveals TABLE SCAN vs INDEX SEARCH. The fix is adding a composite index on the frequently filtered columns — coordinated with the DBA and tested for impact on write performance.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: bseg pe AMDP slow tha — 90 seconds. EXPLAIN PLAN dikhaya TABLE SCAN on bseg. DBA ne confirm kiya — BUKRS + GJAHR + HKONT composite index missing tha. Index add hua — EXPLAIN PLAN phir INDEX SEARCH dikhaya. Runtime 90 seconds se 6 seconds.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Index sirf read performance improve karta hai — write performance (INSERT, UPDATE) slightly impact hota hai. DBA ke saath tradeoff discuss karo before adding index on heavy write tables.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Check existing indexes in HANA
SELECT INDEX_NAME, COLUMN_NAME
FROM INDEX_COLUMNS
WHERE SCHEMA_NAME = CURRENT_SCHEMA
  AND TABLE_NAME = 'BSEG'
ORDER BY INDEX_NAME, POSITION;

" Create composite index (DBA level)
" CREATE INDEX ZBSEG_PERF_IDX
"   ON BSEG (BUKRS, GJAHR, HKONT);

" Verify with EXPLAIN PLAN after index:
EXPLAIN PLAN FOR
SELECT hkont, SUM(dmbtr)
FROM bseg
WHERE bukrs = '1000' AND gjahr = '2024'
GROUP BY hkont;
" Look for: INDEX_NAME = 'ZBSEG_PERF_IDX' in plan

followupAnswerBank.question: What happens when intermediate tabular variables in AMDP become too large?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP mein intermediate tabular variables HANA in-memory resources consume karte hain. Agar variable tens of millions of rows hold karta hai — example, aggregation se pehle bina filters ke BSEG ko BKPF ke saath joining — HANA memory pressure badhti hai, potentially out-of-memory errors ya swap usage ki wajah se significant performance degradation cause karta hai. Fix hai filter-first design: JOINs se pehle SQLScript mein early WHERE conditions apply karo intermediate variable sizes reduce karne ke liye. Jitna jaldi ho aggregate karo intermediate variables mein full detail rows ke bajaye summary data ke saath kaam karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Intermediate tabular variables in AMDP consume HANA in-memory resources. If a variable holds tens of millions of rows — for example, joining BSEG with BKPF without filters before aggregation — HANA memory pressure increases, potentially causing out-of-memory errors or significant performance degradation due to swap usage. The fix is filter-first design: apply WHERE conditions early in the SQLScript to reduce intermediate variable sizes before JOINs. Aggregate as early as possible to work with summary data rather than full detail rows in intermediate variables.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AMDP mein sequence thi: pehle full bseg JOIN bkpf — 10 crore row intermediate variable. Phir filter. HANA memory pressure se job fail hua. Sequence reverse ki — pehle bkpf pe filter, chhota set bana, phir bseg JOIN. Intermediate variable size 10 crore se 50,000 ho gaya — memory issue resolve.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Filter last mein karna anti-pattern hai SQLScript mein. Hamesha pehle restrict karo, phir join karo — yeh HANA memory aur performance dono ke liye best practice hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" BAD: Full join first, then filter - huge intermediate
" lt_all = SELECT b.belnr, s.hkont, s.dmbtr
"          FROM bkpf AS b
"          INNER JOIN bseg AS s ON b.belnr = s.belnr;
" lt_filtered = SELECT * FROM :lt_all
"              WHERE b.bukrs = :iv_bukrs;  " too late

" GOOD: Filter early, then join - small intermediate
METHOD filter_first_amdp
    BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
    USING bkpf bseg.
  " Step 1: filter bkpf first - small result
  lt_docs = SELECT belnr, bukrs FROM bkpf
            WHERE bukrs = :iv_bukrs
              AND gjahr = :iv_gjahr;
  " Step 2: join only filtered docs with bseg
  et_result = SELECT s.hkont, SUM(s.dmbtr) AS bal
              FROM :lt_docs AS d
              INNER JOIN bseg AS s
                ON d.belnr = s.belnr AND d.bukrs = s.bukrs
              GROUP BY s.hkont;
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the impact of calling AMDP inside an ABAP LOOP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ABAP LOOP ke andar AMDP call karna excessive database round-trips create karta hai — har iteration apne own network overhead aur HANA execution setup cost ke saath ek separate HANA stored procedure call trigger karta hai. 1000 iterations wale loop ke liye, iska matlab 1000 stored procedure calls. Solution hai AMDP call ko loop ke bahar move karna — sab input ko table parameter ke roop mein pass karo, single HANA call mein sab data process karo, aur complete result return karo. Alternatively, per iteration AMDP call karne ke bajaye lookups ke liye AMDP result set use karne ke liye ABAP logic restructure karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Calling an AMDP inside an ABAP LOOP creates excessive database round-trips — each iteration triggers a separate HANA stored procedure call with its own network overhead and HANA execution setup cost. For a loop of 1000 iterations, this means 1000 stored procedure calls. The solution is to move the AMDP call outside the loop — pass all input as a table parameter, process all data in a single HANA call, and return the complete result. Alternatively, restructure the ABAP logic to use the AMDP result set for lookups rather than calling AMDP per iteration.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: ABAP program mein company codes ka loop tha — 50 company codes. Har code ke liye AMDP call. 50 HANA roundtrips — 3 seconds each — 150 seconds total. AMDP redesign kiya: table importing parameter accept kare — company codes list — ek call mein sab process kare. 150 seconds se 4 seconds.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: LOOP mein lightweight calculation AMDP bhi expensive ho sakti hai jab cumulative overhead count karo. N+1 problem wahi hai — SQL mein bhi hota hai, AMDP mein bhi.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" BAD: AMDP call in ABAP loop - 50 HANA roundtrips
" LOOP AT lt_bukrs INTO ls_bukrs.
"   lo_amdp->get_data( IMPORTING iv_bukrs = ls_bukrs-bukrs
"                      EXPORTING et_result = lt_part ).
"   APPEND LINES OF lt_part TO lt_all.
" ENDLOOP.

" GOOD: Pass all company codes to AMDP - 1 HANA call
METHOD get_all_company_data
    BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
    USING bseg bkpf.
  " it_bukrs is TABLE type IMPORTING - list of company codes
  et_result = SELECT s.bukrs, s.hkont,
                     SUM(s.dmbtr) AS bal
              FROM bseg AS s
              WHERE s.bukrs IN (SELECT bukrs FROM :it_bukrs)
              GROUP BY s.bukrs, s.hkont;
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do outdated HANA statistics cause performance problems in AMDP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: HANA column statistics use karta hai — row counts, value distributions, cardinality — execution plans decide karne ke liye jaise index use karna ya full scan karna, aur hash join ke build side ke roop mein kaunsi table use karna. Jab data volume statistics update ke bina significantly grow hota hai, optimizer stale estimates ke basis pe decisions leta hai. Yeh isko full table scans choose karne pe cause kar sakta hai jahan index seeks better hote, ya inefficient join order pick karne pe. Fix hai affected tables pe UPDATE STATISTICS run karna — typically DBA ke through ya HANA ke automatic statistics update job ke through.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: HANA uses column statistics — row counts, value distributions, cardinality — to decide execution plans like whether to use an index or do a full scan, and which table to use as the build side of a hash join. When data volume grows significantly without statistics update, the optimizer makes decisions based on stale estimates. This can cause it to choose full table scans where index seeks would be better, or to pick an inefficient join order. The fix is running UPDATE STATISTICS on affected tables — typically done by the DBA or via HANA's automatic statistics update job.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AMDP plan suddenly bad ho gaya — koi code change nahi tha. DBA ne check kiya — bkpf pe statistics 6 months se update nahi hui thi, table 5x grow kar chuki thi. UPDATE STATISTICS RUN ON bkpf kiya — optimizer plan immediately better hua, runtime normal ho gaya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Statistics issue ko index issue samajhna — EXPLAIN PLAN se check karo pehle. Agar index hai lekin plan still full scan dikha raha hai — statistics problem ho sakti hai, index missing nahi.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Check when statistics were last updated
SELECT TABLE_NAME,
       LAST_REFRESH_TIME,
       ROW_COUNT
FROM M_TABLE_STATISTICS
WHERE SCHEMA_NAME = CURRENT_SCHEMA
  AND TABLE_NAME IN ('BSEG', 'BKPF', 'VBAK')
ORDER BY LAST_REFRESH_TIME ASC;

" If LAST_REFRESH_TIME is old -> run statistics update
" UPDATE STATISTICS ON BSEG;  -- DBA runs this

" Check M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS before and after:
SELECT STATEMENT_STRING,
       TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME / EXECUTION_COUNT AS avg_us
FROM M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS
WHERE STATEMENT_STRING LIKE '%BSEG%'
ORDER BY avg_us DESC;

followupAnswerBank.question: What is SELECT * anti-pattern in AMDP and how does it affect performance?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP SQLScript mein SELECT * intermediate tabular variable mein table ke sab columns fetch karta hai. HANA columnar database hai jo specific columns read karne ke liye optimized hai — sirf required columns project karna HANA ko unnecessary column data load karna entirely skip karne deta hai. SELECT * sab columns load karne pe force karta hai, intermediate variables mein memory consumption badhata hai, potentially I/O badhata hai, aur AMDP steps ke beech flow karne wale data volume badhata hai. BSEG (200+ columns) jaisi wide tables ke liye, explicitly 5-10 needed columns list karna intermediate variable memory footprint dramatically reduce kar sakta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SELECT * in AMDP SQLScript fetches all columns from a table into the intermediate tabular variable. HANA is a columnar database optimized for reading specific columns — projecting only required columns allows HANA to skip loading unnecessary column data entirely. SELECT * forces all columns to be loaded, increasing memory consumption in intermediate variables, potentially increasing I/O, and increasing the data volume that flows between AMDP steps. For wide tables like BSEG (200+ columns), explicitly listing the 5-10 needed columns can dramatically reduce intermediate variable memory footprint.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AMDP mein SELECT * FROM bseg tha — intermediate table mein 230 columns, 5 lakh rows — significant memory. Changed to SELECT belnr, bukrs, hkont, dmbtr, gjahr FROM bseg — sirf 5 columns. Memory usage 20x kam hua, runtime bhi improve hua.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Debug ke liye SELECT * temporary rakhna theek hai but production mein hamesha specific columns list karo. Code review mein SELECT * specifically check karo AMDP mein.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" BAD: SELECT * fetches all 200+ columns from BSEG
" lt_items = SELECT * FROM bseg WHERE bukrs = :iv_bukrs;
" Memory: 5 lakh rows x 200 columns = excessive

" GOOD: Only needed columns - HANA columnar advantage
METHOD column_projection_amdp
    BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
    USING bseg.
  " Only 5 columns needed - massive memory saving
  lt_items = SELECT belnr,
                    bukrs,
                    hkont,
                    dmbtr,
                    gjahr
             FROM bseg
             WHERE bukrs = :iv_bukrs
               AND gjahr = :iv_gjahr;
  et_result = SELECT hkont,
                     SUM(dmbtr) AS balance
              FROM :lt_items
              GROUP BY hkont;
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: Can AMDP cause HANA memory issues and how do you handle it?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan, AMDP HANA memory issues cause kar sakta hai. Bade intermediate tabular variables — massive tables ke unfiltered joins — significant HANA in-memory resources consume karte hain. Jab multiple users simultaneously memory-intensive AMDPs run karte hain, contention occur hoti hai. Prevention strategies: intermediate variable size minimize karne ke liye pehle filter karo, full detail rows rakhne ke bajaye early aggregate karo, WHERE conditions ke saath EXPORTING result size limit karo. Batch AMDPs ke liye, online users ke saath memory contention avoid karne ke liye off-peak hours mein schedule karo. Memory trends track karne ke liye M_MEMORY_USAGE aur M_LOAD_HISTORY_HOST monitor karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Yes, AMDP can cause HANA memory issues. Large intermediate tabular variables — unfiltered joins of massive tables — consume significant HANA in-memory resources. When multiple users simultaneously run memory-intensive AMDPs, contention occurs. Prevention strategies: filter first to minimize intermediate variable size, aggregate early rather than keeping full detail rows, limit EXPORTING result size with WHERE conditions. For batch AMDPs, schedule during off-peak hours to avoid memory contention with online users. Monitor M_MEMORY_USAGE and M_LOAD_HISTORY_HOST to track memory trends.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Month-end pe AMDP-based report slow ho jaati thi — sab users ek saath run karte the. M_MEMORY_USAGE check kiya — peak pe HANA memory 85% use ho raha tha. Solution: report ko chhote company code groups mein divide kiya — sequentially run karo, not all at once. Memory pressure normalized.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Memory issue hone pe blindly more servers add karna — root cause SQLScript mein zyaada data process karna hai. Code optimization pehle, scaling baad mein.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Monitor HANA memory usage
SELECT HOST,
       ROUND(USED_PHYSICAL_MEMORY / 1073741824, 1) AS used_gb,
       ROUND(ALLOCATION_LIMIT / 1073741824, 1)     AS limit_gb,
       ROUND(USED_PHYSICAL_MEMORY * 100.0
             / ALLOCATION_LIMIT, 1)                AS pct_used
FROM M_HOST_RESOURCE_UTILIZATION;

" Chunking approach in ABAP caller to avoid memory spike
SELECT bukrs FROM t001 INTO TABLE @lt_bukrs.
LOOP AT lt_bukrs INTO ls_bukrs.
  " AMDP called per company code - not all at once
  lo_amdp->get_data(
    IMPORTING iv_bukrs  = ls_bukrs-bukrs
    EXPORTING et_result = lt_part ).
  APPEND LINES OF lt_part TO lt_all.
ENDLOOP.
" Each iteration has manageable memory footprint

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the impact of unoptimized JOIN conditions in AMDP SQLScript?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP SQLScript mein incorrect ya incomplete JOIN ON conditions cartesian joins cause kar sakti hain — ek table ki har row doosre table ki multiple rows ke saath match karti hai — explosive intermediate result set lead karte hue. Example, sirf BELNR pe BKPF aur BSEG join karna BUKRS include kiye bina potential duplicates create karta hai kyunki BELNR company codes ke across repeat ho sakti hai. Intermediate tabular variable exponentially grow karta hai, memory consume karte hue aur extreme slowness cause karte hue. Fix: hamesha JOIN ON clause mein sab key columns include karo — BKPF/BSEG join ke liye matlab BELNR aur BUKRS dono.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Incorrect or incomplete JOIN ON conditions in AMDP SQLScript can cause cartesian joins — every row from one table matching multiple rows from another — leading to an explosive intermediate result set. For example, joining BKPF and BSEG only on BELNR without including BUKRS creates potential duplicates since BELNR may repeat across company codes. The intermediate tabular variable grows exponentially, consuming memory and causing extreme slowness. Fix: always include all key columns in the JOIN ON clause — for BKPF/BSEG join that means both BELNR and BUKRS.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AMDP mein BKPF JOIN BSEG ON belnr tha — bukrs missing tha ON clause mein. Result mein doubles tha sab totals — cartesian create ho raha tha cross-company. ON clause mein AND d.bukrs = s.bukrs add kiya — totals correct ho gaye, performance bhi improve hua.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: JOIN ke baad result count check karna bhoolna — expected rows se zyada rows = cartesian product signal. COUNT(*) check karo intermediate variables pe during debug.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" BAD: Incomplete JOIN - potential cartesian / duplicates
" lt_joined = SELECT b.belnr, s.hkont, s.dmbtr
"             FROM bkpf AS b
"             INNER JOIN bseg AS s
"               ON b.belnr = s.belnr;  " missing bukrs!

" GOOD: Complete composite key JOIN
METHOD correct_join_amdp
    BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
    USING bkpf bseg.
  lt_joined = SELECT b.belnr, b.bukrs,
                     s.hkont, s.dmbtr
              FROM bkpf AS b
              INNER JOIN bseg AS s
                ON b.belnr = s.belnr
               AND b.bukrs = s.bukrs  " <- essential!
              WHERE b.bukrs = :iv_bukrs
                AND b.gjahr = :iv_gjahr;
  et_result = SELECT hkont, SUM(dmbtr) AS bal
              FROM :lt_joined GROUP BY hkont;
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you profile AMDP performance — what metrics do you look at?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP performance profiling ke liye, main multiple levels pe measure karta hoon. ABAP layer: AMDP call ke before aur after GET TIME STAMP use karke total program runtime. ST05 trace: HANA stored procedure call ko specifically attributed database time versus ABAP processing time. HANA layer: execution count, total time, aur average per-call duration ke liye M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS. Slow SELECT statements ke liye EXPLAIN PLAN — operator name, table access type, aur estimated output size. Debug ke dauran SELECT COUNT(*) use karke intermediate tabular variable row counts. AMDP execution window ke dauran M_HOST_RESOURCE_UTILIZATION mein memory usage trend.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For AMDP performance profiling, I measure at multiple levels. ABAP layer: total program runtime using GET TIME STAMP before and after the AMDP call. ST05 trace: database time specifically attributed to the HANA stored procedure call versus ABAP processing time. HANA layer: M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS for execution count, total time, and average per-call duration. EXPLAIN PLAN for the slow SELECT statements — operator name, table access type, and estimated output size. Intermediate tabular variable row counts using SELECT COUNT(*) during debug. Memory usage trend in M_HOST_RESOURCE_UTILIZATION during the AMDP execution window.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: AMDP optimization karte waqt ek tracking table banaya — program, runtime, timestamp, row count. Har optimization round ke baad entry add ki. Progress clearly visible tha — before 8 min, after index 4 min, after SQLScript rewrite 12 sec. Management ko concrete numbers de sake.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf total program time dekhna — alag karo ki time ABAP mein hai ya HANA mein. ST05 se database time specifically measure karo. Wrong layer optimize karne pe effort waste hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Runtime measurement wrapper
METHOD measure_amdp_perf.
  DATA: lv_start TYPE timestampl,
        lv_end   TYPE timestampl.
  GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_start.

  lo_amdp->get_gl_balance(
    IMPORTING iv_bukrs  = iv_bukrs
              iv_gjahr  = iv_gjahr
    EXPORTING et_result = et_result ).

  GET TIME STAMP FIELD lv_end.
  DATA(lv_elapsed) = cl_abap_tstmp=>subtract(
    tstmp1 = lv_end tstmp2 = lv_start ).
  " Log: lv_elapsed, LINES(et_result), iv_bukrs, iv_gjahr
ENDMETHOD.
" HANA side metric:
" SELECT TOTAL_EXECUTION_TIME/EXECUTION_COUNT AS avg_us
" FROM M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS WHERE ..LIKE 'ZCL_GL%'

amdp-hana-027.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Authorization problem matlab galat user galat data dekh sakta hai — ya sahi user data nahi dekh pa raha. AMDP mein yeh zyada tricky hai kyunki AMDP ke andar normal ABAP authorization check (AUTHORITY-CHECK statement) nahi likhna possible. Isliye agar dhyan nahi rakha toh: ek user jo HR data nahi dekhna chahiye woh AMDP se dekh sakta hai. Ya ek user jo sab company codes ka data dekhna chahiye use only one company code ka data milta hai kyunki ABAP wrapper mein wrong check tha. Teen main authorization problems AMDP mein hote hain. Pehla — ABAP layer pe check hi nahi kiya — AMDP seedha call ho gayi bina authorization ke — unauthorized data access. Doosra — HANA layer pe authorization — HANA stored procedure ko call karne ke liye user ke paas HANA-level EXECUTE permission chahiye — agar nahi hai toh runtime error. Teesra — CDS view access control bypass — agar AMDP directly table se access kare aur CDS view ki row-level security bypass ho jaaye. Yeh teeno real production mein aane wali problems hain.
amdp-hana-027.hinglishMasterExplanation:
AMDP mein authorization issues multiple layers pe occur karte hain. Interview mein layered authorization model clearly explain karo:

1. ABAP Layer Authorization (Missing Check):
Sabse common aur dangerous issue. AMDP ke andar AUTHORITY-CHECK statement allowed nahi hai — pure SQLScript hai. Iska matlab: agar calling ABAP code mein authorization check nahi kiya toh AMDP bina kisi restriction ke data return karti hai. Fix: AMDP call karne wali ABAP method mein explicit AUTHORITY-CHECK karo. Sensitive data ke liye double check: object-level aur field-level authorization.

2. HANA Stored Procedure Execution Rights:
AMDP activate hone ke baad HANA mein ek stored procedure ban jaata hai. ABAP user ke paas us stored procedure pe EXECUTE privilege hona chahiye. Normally SAP framework automatically yeh grant karta hai through ABAP runtime — but kuch configurations mein ya authorization changes ke baad yeh privilege missing ho sakta hai. Runtime error: 'insufficient privilege' ya 'not authorized to execute procedure'. Fix: Basis team se HANA stored procedure execute privilege check karwao aur grant karwao if missing.

3. HANA Database User Authorization:
AMDP HANA pe ABAP runtime user ki taraf se run hota hai — SAPABAP1 ya similar technical user. Agar is user ke paas referenced tables pe SELECT privilege nahi hai — AMDP fail hogi. Basis team HANA user privileges manage karta hai. Symptom: 'insufficient privilege' error specifically for table access.

4. CDS Access Control Bypass:
CDS views mein @AccessControl annotations aur DCL files row-level security enforce karte hain. Agar AMDP directly transparent tables access kare (bseg, vbak) instead of CDS views — CDS DCL rules bypass ho jaati hain. Users woh data dekh sakte hain jo CDS view unhe restrict karti. Fix: AMDP mein direct table access ke badle CDS views access karo USING clause mein — DCL rules automatically apply hongi.

5. Cross-Client Data Exposure:
SAP multi-client system mein MANDT field se client isolation hoti hai. AMDP SQLScript mein agar MANDT filter explicitly nahi lagaya — other client ka data visible ho sakta hai. ABAP Open SQL automatically MANDT filter karta hai — SQLScript mein manually lagana padta hai.

6. Organizational Level Authorization:
Kuch users sirf specific company codes ya sales organizations ke data ke authorized hain. AMDP call karte waqt agar yeh organizational restrictions ABAP layer pe enforce nahi ki toh user zyada data dekh sakta hai. Fix: ABAP mein user ke authorized company codes/sales orgs list retrieve karo, AMDP ke IMPORTING parameters mein pass karo, SQLScript mein IN condition se filter karo.
amdp-hana-027.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'Authorization issues in AMDP occur at multiple layers. The most critical is the missing ABAP authority check — since ABAP AUTHORITY-CHECK cannot be written inside the SQLScript body, the calling ABAP method must explicitly perform all authorization checks before invoking the AMDP. Skipping this is a security vulnerability — the AMDP will return data regardless of the user's business authorization. Second, HANA-level execute privileges on the generated stored procedure must be granted — missing EXECUTE privilege causes a runtime insufficient privilege error. Third, if AMDP accesses transparent tables directly instead of CDS views, CDS DCL row-level access control is bypassed — users may see data they should not. Finally, in multi-client systems, the MANDT filter must be explicitly added in SQLScript since ABAP Open SQL client handling does not apply inside AMDP.'

Hinglish tip: Authorization question mein security awareness dikhana bohot important hai. Interviewer dekhna chahta hai ki tum sirf code likhna jaante ho ya security implications bhi samajhte ho. Bolna: 'AMDP mein authorization miss karna sirf bug nahi hai — yeh security vulnerability hai. Sensitive financial ya HR data unauthorized users ko expose ho sakta hai.' Yeh maturity aur professional awareness dikhata hai.
amdp-hana-027.codeExamples:
" === Authorization Anti-pattern: No check before AMDP ===
" WRONG: AMDP called without any authorization check
" lo_amdp->get_sensitive_data( ... )  " <- DANGEROUS!

" === CORRECT Pattern 1: ABAP authority check before AMDP ===
METHOD get_financial_data_secure.
  " Step 1: Check ABAP authorization object
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    FIELD 'BUKRS' ID iv_bukrs
    FIELD 'ACTVT' ID '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_no_authority
      MESSAGE e001(zamdp_auth).
  ENDIF.
  " Step 2: Only authorized users reach AMDP call
  lo_amdp->get_gl_balance(
    IMPORTING iv_bukrs  = iv_bukrs
              iv_gjahr  = iv_gjahr
    EXPORTING et_result = et_result ).
ENDMETHOD.

" === CORRECT Pattern 2: Pass authorized orgs to AMDP ===
METHOD get_sales_data_with_auth.
  " Build list of user's authorized sales orgs
  DATA lt_auth_vkorg TYPE RANGE OF vkorg.
  " ... AUTHORITY-CHECK loop to fill lt_auth_vkorg ...
  " Pass authorized orgs as filter to AMDP
  lo_amdp->get_sales_by_org(
    IMPORTING it_vkorg  = lt_auth_vkorg
    EXPORTING et_result = et_result ).
ENDMETHOD.
amdp-hana-027.interviewScriptHinglish: AMDP mein authorization handling ko deliberately design karna padta hai kyunki SQLScript body AUTHORITY-CHECK run nahi kar sakti. ABAP wrapper jo AMDP ko call karta hai sab authorization checks perform karta hai, aur MANDT ko explicitly pass karna padta hai kyunki client filtering automatic nahi hai. Sensitive datasets ke liye DCL access roles wale CDS views preferred hain kyunki security model centralised hai aur bypass karna mushkil hai. Hum DBACOCKPIT se HANA-level access bhi audit karte hain aur ensure karte hain ki AMDP execution ke liye koi privileged technical users use na ho. Ek project mein code review ne sales data leak prevent kiya authorization wrapper pattern mandate karke. Business value compliance, audit readiness, aur production mein data leakage prevention hai.
amdp-hana-027.interviewScriptEnglish: Authorization handling in AMDP must be designed deliberately because the SQLScript body cannot run AUTHORITY-CHECK. The ABAP wrapper that calls the AMDP performs all authorization checks, and MANDT must be passed explicitly because client filtering is not automatic. For sensitive datasets, CDS views with DCL access roles are preferred since the security model is centralised and harder to bypass. We also audit HANA-level access through DBACOCKPIT and ensure no privileged technical users are used for AMDP execution. In one project a code review prevented a sales data leak by mandating the authorization wrapper pattern. The business value is compliance, audit readiness, and prevention of data leakage in production.

amdp-hana-027.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Authorization design pattern around AMDP given SQLScript cannot enforce ABAP authority checks.

Used for:
- Securing sensitive reads in finance, HR, and master data
- Meeting compliance and audit requirements
- Preventing cross-client data leakage

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT '...' in ABAP wrapper before AMDP call
- Explicit MANDT filter through IMPORTING parameter
- DCL with pfcg_auth on CDS Table Functions
- DBACOCKPIT for HANA-level audit

Interview keyword: ABAP wrapper checks plus DCL for CDS

followupAnswerBank.question: Why can't you write AUTHORITY-CHECK inside an AMDP method body?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AUTHORITY-CHECK pure ABAP statement hai jo ABAP application server runtime pe execute hota hai. AMDP method body mein sirf SQLScript hai — jo SAP HANA database pe stored procedure ki tarah run hota hai. ABAP runtime AMDP body execution mein participate nahi karta. Isliye, AUTHORITY-CHECK AMDP body ke andar syntactically aur architecturally dono impossible hai — likhne se activation error cause karega. Iska matlab sab authorization logic calling ABAP code mein implement honi chahiye, AMDP method invoked hone se pehle.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: AUTHORITY-CHECK is a pure ABAP statement that executes on the ABAP application server runtime. An AMDP method body contains only SQLScript — which runs on the SAP HANA database as a stored procedure. ABAP runtime does not participate in AMDP body execution. Therefore, AUTHORITY-CHECK is both syntactically and architecturally impossible inside an AMDP body — writing it would cause an activation error. This means all authorization logic must be implemented in the calling ABAP code, before the AMDP method is invoked.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Naya developer AMDP mein AUTHORITY-CHECK likhne ki koshish kiya — immediately activation error aaya. Explain kiya: AMDP body HANA pe jaata hai, ABAP statements wahan invalid hain. Authorization check calling ABAP method mein karo pehle.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Yeh assume karna ki AMDP somehow ABAP authorization context inherit karta hai automatically — nahi karta. Explicit check karna zaroori hai. Yeh common misconception hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" WRONG - activation error: ABAP statement in AMDP body
" METHOD bad_amdp BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
"                   LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING bseg.
"   AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK' ... <- FAILS
" ENDMETHOD.

" CORRECT - authority check in ABAP wrapper
METHOD amdp_wrapper.
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    FIELD 'BUKRS' ID iv_bukrs
    FIELD 'ACTVT' ID '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_no_authority.
  ENDIF.
  " Only authorized call reaches here
  lo_amdp->get_data(
    IMPORTING iv_bukrs = iv_bukrs
    EXPORTING et_result = et_result ).
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What HANA-level permissions does an AMDP require?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP do categories ki HANA permissions require karta hai. Pehla, generated HANA stored procedure pe EXECUTE privilege — yeh ABAP runtime user ke procedure call karne ke liye chahiye. Doosra, USING clause mein listed sab database tables aur views pe SELECT privilege — yeh ABAP runtime ke through use kiye gaye HANA technical user ko granted hone chahiye. Standard SAP S/4HANA installations mein, yeh privileges Basis ke through pre-configured hote hain. Issues transports ke baad arise hote hain jab target systems mein new AMDP procedures create hote hain lekin privileges automatically re-granted nahi hote — insufficient privilege runtime error result karte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: AMDP requires two categories of HANA permissions. First, EXECUTE privilege on the generated HANA stored procedure — this is needed for the ABAP runtime user to call the procedure. Second, SELECT privilege on all database tables and views listed in the USING clause — these must be granted to the HANA technical user used by ABAP runtime. In standard SAP S/4HANA installations, these privileges are pre-configured by Basis. Issues arise after transports when new AMDP procedures are created in target systems but privileges are not automatically re-granted — resulting in an insufficient privilege runtime error.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production transport ke baad AMDP cx_amdp_error de raha tha — message tha 'insufficient privilege'. Basis team ne check kiya — HANA mein new stored procedure create hua tha transport se but EXECUTE privilege SAPABAP1 user ko grant nahi tha. Basis ne manually GRANT EXECUTE ON <procedure> TO SAPABAP1 run kiya — issue resolve.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: HANA privilege issue ko code bug samajhna — error message carefully padho. 'Insufficient privilege' hana authorization issue hai, SQLScript logic issue nahi.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" HANA privilege check (DBA runs these)
" Check stored procedure exists after transport:
SELECT PROCEDURE_NAME, OWNER_NAME
FROM PROCEDURES
WHERE PROCEDURE_NAME LIKE 'ZCL_GL_BALANCE%';

" Check EXECUTE privilege granted:
SELECT GRANTEE, OBJECT_NAME, PRIVILEGE
FROM OBJECT_PRIVILEGES
WHERE OBJECT_NAME LIKE 'ZCL_GL_BALANCE%'
  AND PRIVILEGE = 'EXECUTE';

" If missing, Basis grants:
" GRANT EXECUTE ON ZCL_GL_BALANCE_AMDP__GET_GL_BALANCE
"   TO SAPABAP1;

followupAnswerBank.question: How does AMDP bypass CDS view access control and what is the fix?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CDS views DCL files aur @AccessControl annotations ke through row-level access control enforce karte hain — ABAP authorization objects pe based SQL conditions automatically har CDS query mein append hoti hain. Jab AMDP apne USING clause aur SQLScript body mein directly transparent tables access karta hai, yeh CDS DCL layer entirely bypass hota hai — HANA stored procedure bina kisi CDS-level restrictions ke directly table se read karta hai. Fix: jahan row-level security required hai, USING clause mein direct tables ke bajaye CDS views reference karo. Alternatively, ABAP mein user ka authorized scope determine karo aur SQLScript mein filter karne ke liye AMDP ko IMPORTING parameters ke roop mein pass karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS views enforce row-level access control through DCL files and @AccessControl annotations — SQL conditions based on ABAP authorization objects are automatically appended to every CDS query. When an AMDP accesses transparent tables directly in its USING clause and SQLScript body, this CDS DCL layer is bypassed entirely — the HANA stored procedure reads directly from the table without any CDS-level restrictions. The fix: reference CDS views in the USING clause instead of direct tables where row-level security is required. Alternatively, determine the user's authorized scope in ABAP and pass it as IMPORTING parameters to the AMDP to filter in SQLScript.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Security audit mein paya gaya ki AMDP directly vbak table access kar raha tha. CDS view ZI_SALESORDER_SECURE pe DCL tha jo VKORG filter karta tha. AMDP CDS view bypass kar raha tha — sab users sab sales orgs dekh sakte the. USING mein vbak ki jagah zi_salesorder_secure use kiya — DCL automatically apply hone lagi.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Yeh assume karna ki ABAP layer pe CDS view use karna aur AMDP mein direct table use karna same security result deta hai — nahi deta. AMDP SQLScript mein object access independently evaluate hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" BAD: Direct table access - bypasses CDS DCL security
" METHOD get_sales_bypass
"     BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
"     LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
"     USING vbak.  " <- no DCL applies here
"   et_result = SELECT * FROM vbak;  " all sales orgs visible
" ENDMETHOD.

" GOOD: CDS view access - DCL row-level security applies
METHOD get_sales_secure
    BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
    LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
    USING zi_salesorder_secure.  " <- CDS with DCL
  " DCL automatically filters by user's authorized VKORG
  et_result = SELECT vbeln, vkorg, kunnr, netwr
              FROM zi_salesorder_secure
              WHERE erdat >= :iv_from_date;
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle MANDT (client) filtering in AMDP SQLScript?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SAP MANDT field ko multi-client system mein client isolation ke liye use karta hai. ABAP Open SQL automatically har query mein client condition append karta hai — developers ko likhne ki zarurat nahi. Lekin AMDP ke andar HANA SQLScript ko is automatic client filtering ka benefit nahi milta — stored procedure directly table access karta hai. Agar SQLScript WHERE clause mein MANDT explicitly filtered nahi hai, sab clients se data return ho sakta hai. Fix hai current client ko IMPORTING scalar parameter ke through pass karna — typically calling ABAP se SY-MANDT — aur SQLScript body mein har table access pe MANDT = :iv_mandt add karna.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SAP uses the MANDT field for client isolation in a multi-client system. ABAP Open SQL automatically appends the client condition to every query — developers do not need to write it. However, HANA SQLScript inside an AMDP does not benefit from this automatic client filtering — the stored procedure directly accesses the table. If MANDT is not explicitly filtered in the SQLScript WHERE clause, data from all clients may be returned. The fix is to pass the current client via an IMPORTING scalar parameter — typically SY-MANDT from the calling ABAP — and add MANDT = :iv_mandt to every table access in the SQLScript body.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Development system mein do clients the — 100 aur 200. AMDP mein MANDT filter nahi tha. Client 100 se report run kiya — client 200 ka data bhi aa raha tha. Immediately MANDT = :iv_mandt add kiya har table access pe. ABAP caller mein iv_mandt = sy-mandt pass kiya. Issue fix.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Single-client system pe test karne ke baad MANDT issue miss ho jaata hai — multi-client environment mein aake expose hota hai. Hamesha explicitly MANDT filter karo SQLScript mein — defensive coding.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" BAD: No MANDT filter - all clients visible
" lt_docs = SELECT belnr, bukrs FROM bkpf
"           WHERE bukrs = :iv_bukrs;  " missing MANDT!

" GOOD: Explicit MANDT filtering
CLASS zcl_safe_amdp DEFINITION PUBLIC.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_amdp_marker_hdb.
    METHODS get_docs
      IMPORTING VALUE(iv_mandt) TYPE mandt  " pass sy-mandt
                VALUE(iv_bukrs) TYPE bukrs
      EXPORTING VALUE(et_result) TYPE tt_result.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_safe_amdp IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_docs
      BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB
      LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY
      USING bkpf bseg.
    lt_docs = SELECT belnr, bukrs FROM bkpf
              WHERE mandt = :iv_mandt  " explicit client filter
                AND bukrs = :iv_bukrs;
    et_result = SELECT d.belnr, s.hkont, s.dmbtr
                FROM :lt_docs AS d
                INNER JOIN bseg AS s
                  ON s.mandt = :iv_mandt  " bseg also filtered
                 AND s.belnr = d.belnr
                 AND s.bukrs = d.bukrs;
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you implement organizational-level authorization in AMDP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP mein organizational-level authorization bridge pattern use karta hai. Calling ABAP code mein, user ke liye sab authorized organizational values determine karo — possible company codes, sales organizations, ya plants iterate karo aur permitted values ki list build karne ke liye AUTHORITY-CHECK use karo. Is list ko table-typed IMPORTING parameter ke roop mein AMDP ko pass karo. SQLScript ke andar, IN subquery use karke main data ko is authorized list ke against filter karo: WHERE bukrs IN (SELECT bukrs FROM :it_auth_bukrs). Yeh CDS DCL pe rely kiye bina ABAP authorization objects ko HANA-level filtering pe bridge karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Organizational-level authorization in AMDP uses a bridge pattern. In the calling ABAP code, determine all organizational values the user is authorized for — iterate over possible company codes, sales organizations, or plants and use AUTHORITY-CHECK to build a list of permitted values. Pass this list as a table-typed IMPORTING parameter to the AMDP. Inside SQLScript, filter the main data using an IN subquery against this authorized list: WHERE bukrs IN (SELECT bukrs FROM :it_auth_bukrs). This bridges ABAP authorization objects to HANA-level filtering without relying on CDS DCL.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Finance AMDP report tha. Users ke paas different company code authorizations thi based on F_BKPF_BUK. ABAP wrapper mein loop kiya all T001 company codes pe — AUTHORITY-CHECK F_BKPF_BUK se filter kiya — authorized list bani. AMDP mein TABLE IMPORTING it_auth_bukrs pass kiya. SQLScript mein WHERE bukrs IN (SELECT bukrs FROM :it_auth_bukrs). Har user sirf apne authorized company codes ka data dekh pa raha tha.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf calling parameter pe trust karna — agar user directly API call kare different bukrs ke saath toh? Always server-side ABAP authority check karo — client-provided parameters pe sirf trust mat karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Step 1: Build authorized org list in ABAP
METHOD get_authorized_bukrs
    RETURNING VALUE(rt_bukrs) TYPE tt_bukrs_range.
  DATA lt_all_bukrs TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bukrs.
  SELECT bukrs FROM t001 INTO TABLE @lt_all_bukrs.
  LOOP AT lt_all_bukrs INTO DATA(ls_bukrs).
    AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
      FIELD 'BUKRS' ID ls_bukrs
      FIELD 'ACTVT' ID '03'.
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      APPEND VALUE #( sign = 'I' option = 'EQ'
                      low  = ls_bukrs ) TO rt_bukrs.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.
" Step 2: AMDP with authorized list
" WHERE bukrs IN (SELECT bukrs FROM :it_auth_bukrs)

followupAnswerBank.question: What authorization error can a user face when calling AMDP and how is it resolved?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Users AMDP ke saath do categories ke authorization errors face kar sakte hain. Pehla, ABAP-level authorization failure — calling ABAP wrapper cx_no_authority raise karta hai jab AUTHORITY-CHECK fail karta hai. Resolution hai SU01 ya role ke through user ko required ABAP authorization object values grant karna. Doosra, HANA-level insufficient privilege — 'insufficient privilege' message contain karne wale cx_amdp_error — matlab HANA runtime user ke paas stored procedure pe EXECUTE ya referenced table pe SELECT nahi hai. Basis team HANA privileges grant karke resolve karta hai. Teesra silent issue: authorization filters bina error ke empty ya partial results return karte hain — user apne restricted scope ke liye no data dekhta hai, authorization object assignments ki investigation require karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Users can face two categories of authorization errors with AMDP. First, ABAP-level authorization failure — the calling ABAP wrapper raises cx_no_authority when AUTHORITY-CHECK fails. Resolution is granting the required ABAP authorization object values to the user via SU01 or role. Second, HANA-level insufficient privilege — cx_amdp_error with message containing 'insufficient privilege' — meaning the HANA runtime user lacks EXECUTE on the stored procedure or SELECT on a referenced table. Basis team resolves by granting the HANA privileges. A third silent issue: authorization filters return empty or partial results without error — the user sees no data for their restricted scope, requiring investigation of authorization object assignments.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: User ne complaint ki — report empty aa rahi hai. cx_amdp_error bhi nahi. ABAP wrapper mein company code authorization check tha — user ke paas F_BKPF_BUK authorization nahi thi for any company code. AUTHORITY-CHECK fail hua, exception raised, empty result returned. SU01 mein role assign kiya with company code authorization — report data aane laga.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Empty report ko AMDP bug samajhna — authorization filter cause ho sakti hai. User ke roles aur authorizations pehle check karo SU01 mein, phir code investigate karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Complete authorization error handling pattern
METHOD get_report_with_auth.
  " 1. Check ABAP authorization
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    FIELD 'BUKRS' ID iv_bukrs
    FIELD 'ACTVT' ID '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    " ABAP auth failure - user missing role
    " Resolution: SU01 -> role with F_BKPF_BUK
    RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_no_authority.
  ENDIF.

  TRY.
    lo_amdp->get_gl_balance(
      IMPORTING iv_bukrs  = iv_bukrs
                iv_gjahr  = iv_gjahr
      EXPORTING et_result = et_result ).
  CATCH cx_amdp_error INTO DATA(lx_err).
    " HANA privilege failure
    " Resolution: Basis grants HANA EXECUTE privilege
    DATA(lv_msg) = lx_err->get_text( ).
    RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_hana_auth
      EXPORTING text = lv_msg.
  ENDTRY.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you audit who accessed sensitive data through AMDP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: AMDP-based sensitive data access auditing ABAP-side logging require karta hai kyunki AMDP khud koi built-in audit capability nahi rakhta. Recommended approach hai calling ABAP wrapper method mein log karna: BAL_LOG_MSG_ADD use karke user ID (sy-uname), timestamp, aur accessed parameters (company code, fiscal year, etc.) ko custom audit table ya SAP Application Log mein record karna. HANA side pe, ek HANA Audit Policy stored procedure executions log karne ke liye configured ho sakti hai — database-level audit trail provide karte hue. Regulatory compliance ke liye, ABAP aur HANA dono audit trails milke comprehensive coverage provide karte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Auditing AMDP-based sensitive data access requires ABAP-side logging since AMDP itself has no built-in audit capability. The recommended approach is to log in the calling ABAP wrapper method: record user ID (sy-uname), timestamp, and accessed parameters (company code, fiscal year, etc.) to a custom audit table or SAP Application Log using BAL_LOG_MSG_ADD. On the HANA side, a HANA Audit Policy can be configured to log stored procedure executions — providing a database-level audit trail. For regulatory compliance, both ABAP and HANA audit trails together provide comprehensive coverage.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: HR payroll AMDP tha — auditors require karte the ki kiske paas access tha kab. ABAP wrapper mein ek INSERT to ZAUDLOG table add kiya — uname, timestamp, accessed pernr range, bukrs. Monthly audit report is table se generate hota tha. Auditors satisfied the.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Audit logging AMDP body ke andar karne ki koshish karna — not possible in SQLScript. Logging strictly ABAP caller mein karo. Aur audit log table khud ka data ko AMDP se access nahi karna — circular dependency.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Audit logging pattern in ABAP wrapper
METHOD get_payroll_secure.
  " 1. Authorization check
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'P_ORGIN' ...
  IF sy-subrc <> 0. RAISE EXCEPTION ... ENDIF.

  " 2. Log access for audit trail
  INSERT INTO zhr_access_log VALUES @(
    VALUE #(
      uname     = sy-uname
      timestamp = cl_abap_tstmp=>utclong2tstmpl(
                    utclong_current( ) )
      bukrs     = iv_bukrs
      gjahr     = iv_gjahr
      prog      = sy-repid ) ).

  " 3. Call AMDP after logging
  lo_amdp->get_payroll_data(
    IMPORTING iv_bukrs  = iv_bukrs
              iv_gjahr  = iv_gjahr
    EXPORTING et_result = et_result ).
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the risk of running AMDP with a highly privileged HANA technical user?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ABAP-HANA connection ek technical HANA user use karta hai jaise SAPABAP1. Agar is user ke paas broad privileges hain — sab tables pe SELECT — yeh kisi bhi ABAP authorization gap ke liye amplifier ki tarah act karta hai. Agar AMDP bina proper ABAP authority checks ke called hai, highly privileged technical user silently unauthorized ABAP user ki taraf se sensitive data access karta hai. Mitigation hai principle of least privilege: HANA technical user ke paas system ke liye needed sab SAP application tables pe sirf SELECT hona chahiye, administrative tables ya confidential payroll/HR tables pe nahi without explicit approval. Basis team ke saath periodic privilege reviews scheduled hone chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The ABAP-HANA connection uses a technical HANA user such as SAPABAP1. If this user has broad privileges — SELECT on all tables — it acts as an amplifier for any ABAP authorization gap. If an AMDP is called without proper ABAP authority checks, the highly privileged technical user silently accesses sensitive data on behalf of the unauthorized ABAP user. The mitigation is principle of least privilege: the HANA technical user should have SELECT only on SAP application tables needed for the system, not administrative tables or confidential payroll/HR tables without explicit approval. Periodic privilege reviews should be scheduled with the Basis team.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Security assessment mein mila ki SAPABAP1 user ke paas PA* tables (HR) pe SELECT tha but HR AMDP ke liye ABAP wrapper mein P_ORGIN authority check missing tha. Any user could call AMDP via custom program aur HR data dekh sakta tha. Dual fix: ABAP wrapper mein authority check add, aur Basis se PA* table access restrict karvaya for non-HR AMDPs.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: HANA technical user privileges ko 'SAP ke standard' samajhna aur review na karna — customization mein extra privileges aa sakte hain. Regular audit zaroori hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Check HANA technical user privileges (DBA/Basis runs)
SELECT GRANTEE,
       OBJECT_NAME,
       PRIVILEGE
FROM OBJECT_PRIVILEGES
WHERE GRANTEE = 'SAPABAP1'
  AND OBJECT_TYPE = 'TABLE'
  AND OBJECT_NAME LIKE 'PA%'  -- HR tables
ORDER BY OBJECT_NAME;

" If unnecessary HR table access found -> revoke
" REVOKE SELECT ON PA0001 FROM SAPABAP1;

" Principle of least privilege:
" SAPABAP1 should have SELECT only on tables
" required for productive ABAP processes
" Not on all SAP tables or schema-wide SELECT
